name: Glenn Miller
description: American band leader, arranger, and composer (1904–1944)
abstract: Alton Glen "Glenn" Miller (March 1, 1904 – December 15, 1944) was an American big band conductor, arranger, composer, trombonist, and recording artist before and during World War II, when he was an officer in the US Army Air Forces. His civilian band, Glenn Miller and his Orchestra, was one of the most popular and successful bands of the…

about_quotes.speaker: Ignacio Berroa
about_quotes.evidence: once the Nat King Cole album was played he put on Glenn Miller. And when I heard that I said [scats “In the Mood”] when I heard that I said confirm! Confirm! That’s the music that I want to play
about_quotes.doc_title: Ignacio Berroa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 24, 2021 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Grosz
about_quotes.evidence: when Bobby Hackett played with Glenn Miller, Glenn Miller had him, Bobby didn’t have to carry it, the band boy had to carry an amp all over the place, but Bobby never used it, he didn’t like it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Grosz interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Probably you know, Glen Miller, and Harry James and some of those bands that were recording for the Chesterfield cigarettes’ fifteen-minute program. My mother and I used to listen to them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John LaPorta
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Was there a noticeable difference at the time between let’s say the bands like Glenn Miller and the white oriented bands and Basie and Ellington that you can talk about?
about_quotes.doc_title: John LaPorta interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: He said, “in the 1930s I was there with George Gershwin two rows over, and we had Glenn Miller, and so many musicians. They were studying this way of making their music fresh”
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Houston Person Part 2
about_quotes.evidence: Even if it’s Glenn Miller, Benny Goodman, and all those people, Count Basie and Duke Ellington, listen to those regularly as kids are starting to listen to that
about_quotes.doc_title: Houston Person Part 2 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998 [interview 2 of 2]
